title: Responsive Header with 3-Level Navigation Menu

requirements.text: Create a full-width responsive header component that spans 100% of the viewport width at all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop), with appropriate padding and a consistent height.
acceptanceCriteria: Header stretches to 100% viewport width on all screen sizes
Header maintains consistent height and does not overflow horizontally
Header is visually distinct with a background color/style that contrasts with page content

requirements.text: Implement a 3-level navigation menu in the header for desktop views. Level 1 items appear as top-level links in the horizontal nav bar. Hovering a Level 1 item reveals a Level 2 dropdown. Hovering a Level 2 item reveals a Level 3 flyout or nested sub-menu. Use sample/demo menu data to populate all three levels.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 Level 1 navigation items are visible in the header on desktop
Hovering a Level 1 item opens a dropdown showing Level 2 items
Hovering a Level 2 item opens a nested Level 3 sub-menu (flyout or indented)
Menus close when the user moves the cursor away
All three levels are keyboard-accessible (Tab + Enter/Space/Escape)

requirements.text: On mobile and tablet breakpoints, collapse the 3-level navigation into a hamburger menu icon. Tapping the icon opens a full-width drawer or accordion-style vertical menu. Each level should be expandable/collapsible via tap, showing all three menu levels inline.

quotes: show me header width responsive and 3 level menu

acceptanceCriteria: A hamburger icon appears in the header on screens narrower than the desktop breakpoint
Tapping the hamburger icon opens the mobile menu panel
Level 1 items are listed vertically; tapping one expands Level 2 items below it
Tapping a Level 2 item expands Level 3 items below it
A close button or second tap of the hamburger collapses the menu

requirements.text: Apply a creative, coherent visual theme to the header and navigation menus. Use a bold, modern design with strong typography, a rich dark or jewel-toned color palette (not blue or purple), clear hover/active states, smooth CSS transitions on dropdowns, and consistent spacing throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Header uses a visually distinct non-blue/non-purple color scheme consistently
Dropdown and flyout menus have smooth open/close transitions
Hover and active states are clearly visible for all nav items at all levels
Typography is bold and readable at all breakpoints

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
All navigation state management must use React state (no external state libraries beyond React Query)
nonGoals: Backend data persistence for menu items — use hardcoded demo data
Animated page transitions or routing beyond demonstrating the nav structure
